meta.title: GwangMin Jeon | Service Planner Turning Data & AI into Decisions
meta.description: Portfolio of GwangMin Jeon, a service planner who builds information where none existed and connects it to real decisions.

hero.name: And a curious 'Human API' — GwangMin Jeon.
hero.title: A writer who codes, an engineer who writes.
hero.tagline: I build information where none exists, and connect it to services that change how people decide and act.

profile.lead: Someone who thinks through to where people actually use it
profile.body: I care more about how a technology reaches people than about the technology itself. That's the thread running from my early work with data on people with mobility challenges, through vocational rehabilitation and policy research, to data analysis, AI, and backend development — all in pursuit of solutions that reach more people. I put more value in the change a technology can create than in simply learning it.

about.heading: How I Solve Problems
about.intro: I break working with data into three steps: build it when it doesn't exist, keep the person who'll use it in mind while building it, and push the result all the way through to a form that actually reaches them. That's been my approach for two years and seven months as a national R&D researcher, and through my AI graduate studies and Microsoft AI School.

highlights.title: Build it myself when it's missing
highlights.body: I don't treat missing data as a reason I can't start. When I worked on the autonomous driving research, the pilot region had no related data at all, so I built baseline data through direct field surveys, combined it with three years of usage data, and ran spatial analysis. That became the evidence used to select the pilot routes.

highlights.title: Always think of the person first
highlights.body: Behind every number, there's someone actually living it. For the low-floor bus standard model research, I surveyed roughly 5,000 users directly about their service needs, and the data showed that low usage came from a lack of infrastructure, not a lack of demand. That finding led to the decision to keep the number of wheelchair-accessible seats unchanged.

highlights.title: Turn it into something people can use
highlights.body: Even good insight struggles to create value if it's hard to use. That's why I turned prediction models into dashboards and policy simulators, and receipt data into an automatic structuring feature. I care as much about how well something is delivered as about how accurate it is.

experience.org: Korea Association of Persons with Physical Disabilities (KAPPD) · Institute for Living and Environment of Persons with Disabilities
experience.summary: Led national R&D projects on transportation for people with disabilities, owning the full research process from data collection to analysis, visualization, and reporting.

projects.title: Autonomous Special Transport Vehicle for People with Disabilities
bullets: Collected and cleaned field survey data and built it into a database
Derived mobility patterns of transportation-disadvantaged users through QGIS-based spatial analysis and visualization
Selected candidate pilot routes for autonomous driving based on the analysis

projects.title: Standard Model for Regional Low-Floor Buses
bullets: Researched operating technology for the low-floor seated bus standard model
Planned educational content by collecting, classifying, and comparing domestic and international research and indicators
Planned and ran a competition related to low-floor buses
Analyzed legislation and drafted amendments to mandate related training

experience.footnote: Delivered research outcomes for each project, including published papers and reports

projects.title: AI-Based Travel Expense Tracker 'NOTO'
projects.subtitle: Microsoft AI School Team Project (2nd & Final) · Public launch planned H1 2027
bullets: Proposed "budget management difficulty while traveling" as the shared problem and led planning of the core service concept (expense tracking app)
Built a receipt OCR parsing and structuring module using Azure Document Intelligence and GPT-4o Vision
Implemented Azure Cosmos DB integration with full CRUD data operations
Built a spend-analysis engine producing category spend ratios, budget-overrun alerts, and daily trends
Built a FastAPI-based REST API server (6 endpoints) and deployed to Azure App Service / Static Web Apps
Designed and ran the full extract → transform → load → analyze data pipeline as a team project

projects.title: School-Zone Child Accident Risk Prediction Model
projects.subtitle: Microsoft AI School Team Project (1st)
bullets: Proposed the original concept — a diagnostic service for child-friendly cities based on infrastructure data — which the team refined into accident-risk prediction
Built a prediction model combining safety facility and accident data from 142 school zones in Seongnam and Gwangmyeong with roughly 4,000 road-view images
Owned modeling and validation using Logistic Regression (L1/Lasso, SMOTE, OOF cross-validation)
Built a Streamlit/Folium dashboard to visualize results and a policy simulator to analyze the impact of adding facilities

projects.title: Autonomous Special Transport Vehicle for People with Disabilities
projects.subtitle: KAPPD · National R&D Project · Jun 2023 – Dec 2025
bullets: Collected and cleaned field survey data and built it into a database
Derived mobility patterns of transportation-disadvantaged users through QGIS-based spatial analysis and visualization
Selected candidate pilot routes for autonomous driving based on the analysis

bullets: Completed 7 courses covering AI fundamentals and applying AI to brainstorming, research, writing, content creation, data analysis, and app building
Built a portfolio of 20+ AI-assisted artifacts and vibe-coded a custom AI solution

courses.name: Microsoft AI School, 9th Cohort
bullets: Completed courses in Python data analysis, data collection/preprocessing, and cloud-based machine learning & deep learning
Completed hands-on projects in Azure Machine Learning and Azure OpenAI
Planned and participated in the 1st, 2nd, and final team projects

bullets: Completed a hands-on data analysis course using Pandas, NumPy, and Seaborn
